ads.author: Dirk Smith Johnson
ads.title: Rare Williams OXO Pinball Machine
ads.description: Very rare Williams OXO pinball machine. Previously featured at the Roanoke Pinball Museum in Virginia. This is a unique electro-mechanical game that plays tic-tac-toe! This game has been in fully working condition until recently when it started blowing a fuse during game restart. It's most likely a switch contact somewhere that's broken, but I've been too busy to diagnose it, and I need the room, so I'm willing to sell it for less than I paid to someone who wants a unique game, and is willing to tinker with it. This game is basically in original condition except for being upgraded with $200+ worth of LEDs to make it brighter and more reliable.

ads.author: Berto Moon
ads.title: Bally Night Rider Pinball
ads.description: This weekend special before it goes to storage on Sunday $650 Bally Night Rider Project? Picked it up a little while ago, started getting parts for it and working on it a little bit. So far I put a new Weebly MPU in it. Worked on the rectifier board a bit, it should be replaced or replace the bridge that is bad (they are the smaller bridges not the normal size ones) we jumped one bridge to the other and got most everything to work for testing. Bought new pop caps, new clear plastic protectors, power supply cap kit, orginal backglass was broken so I got a new translite and glass (glass and translite are to thicker than orginal channels. will need to replace them or get a thinner piece of glass I got 1/4 inch glass) A couple displays where not perfect might be the power supply or connectors also. Moving the displays around got them to work in different locations if I remember correctly. Left flipper coil is burned up, I might have a used one I could toss in. That's where I stopped didn't look at the driver board so test the transistor for it. I put it away for new years and now don't feel like messing with it for a while so checking to see if anyone is interested in it. Lockdown bar is included just wasn't in the Pic. Located in Acworth GA

ads.author: Carl Young
ads.title: Arcade cabinets
ads.description: Several arcade machines in various conditions and functionality- Shoot Away II: seems to boot and function, but could not test fully because the rifles are damaged and do no work, and the screen takes up too much space so consider it not functional the screen is missing the feel for it, but it's light so a few screws could hold that screen up. Lethal Enforcers: pretty much fully working, the only issues is the heavy damage to the cabinet itself, and the player 1 and 2 start buttons are broken and would need replaced, I just didn't get that far yet Sega Turbo OutRun: This one has a removed screen and does not boot at all. There's power too it though and there is visual but it's garbled graphics. Lock-on by data east: This one has a dead screen and does not boot at all with a hardware error 29. Cobra Command (inside an atari cabinet?): Has a dead screen and does not boot at all. There's power too it though. Looking to try to get $500 per cabinet but any reasonable offer would be considered. Looking to sell outrun and the monitor separately

Definitely fried) Cab exterior (pretty beat-up overall) Please reach out with offers, on parts or the whole cab! Local pick-up preferred.
